observations.recommendation: The Municipal Accountant revisits COA Circular No. 2023-008 and follow the procedures for the proper disposition of dormant accounts in order to fairly present the accounts in the financial statement.

The Municipal Treasurer instructs the personnel responsible in preparing the RCI to transmit the report together with the related disbursement vouchers and supporting documents of all released checks on a daily basis to allow the Accounting Unit to record the transactions promptly.

Management submits the cash advance voucher and supporting documents to COA Audit Team Leader (ATL) not later than 7 working days after the release of check for post audit in adherence to Section 6.1.8.4 and 6.3 of DBM-DILG-DND & COA Joint Circular No. 2015-01.

The Municipal Mayor instructs the Inventory Committee to place the markings “FOR OFFICIAL USE ONLY” under which is the name of the municipality on each side of the vehicles owned by the LGU in adherence to COA Circular No. 75-6.

Management, in coordination with the Human Resource Management Officer prioritizes and fast tracks the hiring of the required staff in research and planning to a regular position in compliance with NDRRMC-DILG-DBM-CSC Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2014-1.

The Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer prioritize tracing the transactions related to the subsidiary ledger balance recorded as PDAF in prior years’ records to ascertain its nature and if confirmed as PDAF, return the unutilized balance to the Bureau of Treasury.
Management causes investigation on the abnormal balances pertaining to the Due to BIR, Due to GSIS and Due to Pag-IBIG Accounts, and impose appropriate measures to correct them.

Management requires the Municipal Accountant to conduct investigation/ review/examination on the subsidiary ledgers comprising the various liability accounts with negative balances and prepare the necessary adjustments where necessary.

The Municipal Treasurer furnishes the Municipal Accountant with the certified list showing the names of taxpayers and the amounts due and collectible pursuant to Section 20 of the NGAS Manual.  

The Municipal Accountant sets-up the RPT/SET Receivables based on the certified list in order to present reliable balances of accounts in the financial statements.

The Municipal Accountant traces the unaccounted difference of RPT and SET receivables at year-end and make necessary adjustments where necessary in order to present reliable and accurate balances in the financial statements.

Management prepares and submits the Monthly Report of Official Travels for every vehicle and the Report of Fuel Consumption for evaluation.  

Management submits a copy of the specific internal control measures installed in connection with the disbursements for fuel, oil and lubricants expenses.

Management requires the Inventory Committee to prioritize and complete the physical inventory within a reasonable period in accordance with COA Circular No. 2020-006. 

Management ensures the full implementation of “One-time cleansing of PPE accounts” as provided for in the same Circular.

The Local Chief Executive assigns a committee to undertake proper disposal of unserviceable assets in accordance with COA Circular No. 89-296 and 2020-006 so that these will be derecognized in the books of the agency.

Management requires the Inventory Committee to prioritize and complete the physical inventory and reconcile with the property and accounting records, guided by the provisions of COA Circular No. 2020-006, in order to present reliable PPE balances that are verifiable as to existence, condition and accountability.

Management directs the Property Officer to further trace the status of the reported missing/lost assets and if warranted, send demand letters to the concerned accountable officer/personnel for the recovery of the same.  

The Municipal Accountant effects appropriate entries to record receivables and that management refer to COA Circular 2020-006 for further guidance in the disposition of non-existing/missing PPEs.

Management submits the corresponding approved Program of Works to support fuel consumption of heavy equipment during CY 2021.  

Henceforth, we recommend that management strengthen internal control measures for fuel and gasoline consumption by having the duly approved Program of Works as the basis in granting requests to refuel heavy equipment used in various infrastructure projects and that these be attached in the payments for gasoline expenses.

Management submits justification to the Audit Team of this noted deficiency, which is a violation of law (RA 4136).  

Management assigns personnel to ensure that all motor vehicles owned by the LGU properly bear and display government plate numbers in accordance with Section 18 of RA 4136 or the Land Transportation and Traffic Code.
Cause the immediate refund of the cash incentives and enrolment bonus given to municipal officials and employees.
Require the concerned municipal officials to liquidate the said cash advances and refund the excess travel expenses.

Management advises the Municipal Treasurer to conduct an ocular inspection of all land properties used and owned by the municipal government, reconcile this with the available documents to establish their actual existence and ownership as well as the validity of the documents.
Require refund of the amount spent for the travel from the concerned officials and from those authorizing the travel.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant review the Journal Entry Vouchers before posting in the books to ensure that transactions are recorded in accordance with the Chart of Accounts in order to present the correct balances in the financial statements.
Further, we recommended that the Municipal Accountant effect the following proposed adjusting journal entries:
PAJE No. 1 – To reclass the misstated accounts

We recommended and  the Municipal Accountant agreed to adjust/reclassify the above transactions to its appropriate accounts in conformity with the New Chart of Accounts under COA Circular 2015-009:
Other Professional Services			    ₱1,171,800.00
Other MOOE							₱1,171,800.00

We recommended that  the Municipal Accountant thoroughly review the bank reconciliation statements to ensure that only valid reconciling items are included, thereby ensuring the accuracy of the reported Cash in Bank account balances.

We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Accountant to transfer all the unexpended/unobligated balances of 2024 QRF and DRRMF-Mitigation Fund (MOOE) to the Trust Fund Books for easy monitoring of the utilization and to ensure the availability of this fund to support disaster risk reduction and management activities of LDRRMC.

Further, the following journal entries are proposed to be taken up in the books:
Adjusting Entry No. 1 – General Fund Books
Adjusting Entry No. 2 – Trust Fund Books
Adjusting Entry No. 3 – General Fund Books (upon issuance of check)
Adjusting Entry No. 4 – Trust Fund Books (upon issuance of check)

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant trace the cause of the negative balances of the Due to GSIS and Due to Pag-IBIG accounts, reconcile the balances and make the necessary adjustments if warranted.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant make immediate action to review and rectify the erroneous taxes withheld and facilitate refund for the excess of the taxes withheld over the tax due. We further recommended that the Municipal Accountant review the entries made related to withholding tax and remittances for compensation, and prepare the necessary adjusting journal entries to correct the erroneous tax recordings.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant take immediate action to review the entries related to withholding tax and remittances for suppliers/service providers, rectify the erroneous tax remittances, and prepare the necessary adjusting journal entries to correct the erroneous tax recordings. We further recommended that the Municipal Accountant conduct regular and thorough reconciliations between the taxes withheld on supplier payments and the amounts to be remitted, ensuring that all withheld taxes are properly accounted for and accurately remitted to the BIR.
We recommended that Management substantiate for the missing items, demand for their immediate delivery and compute the liquidated damages, when warranted.

We recommended and  the Acting Municipal Engineer agreed to place a signboard on all on-going infrastructure projects of the municipality and submit pictures of the signboards, already with the complete and appropriate data as required by the foregoing circular, for further evaluation and re-inspection by the Audit Team.

We recommended and the Municipal Engineer and MPDC agreed to ensure complete, detailed, and updated reporting on all projects, including descriptions, mode of procurement and specific timelines to improve transparency and accountability on the PPAs undertaken by the agency.

We recommended that Management thoroughly evaluate the request for time extensions, and variation orders guided by the provisions of Sections 1.1, 11 & 39.6, Annex E of RA 9184. Further, fast track the implementation of the aforementioned projects, closely monitor the progress and enforce applicable remedies for unjustified delays to avoid further setbacks.

We recommended that Management, in coordination with the Sangguniang Bayan, direct the Human Resource Management Assistant to formulate and implement comprehensive policy guidelines relative to the compensation of JO personnel to establish clear parameters for determining wage rates based on qualifications, responsibilities, and job complexity, subject to the municipality’s financial capacity.

We recommended that the management discontinue the practice of granting custody and disbursement of public funds to unbonded officers and ensure that all Accountable Officers (AOs) are bonded prior to assuming their roles, in compliance with Section 305(f) of R.A. 7160 and COA Circular 97-002.

We recommended that the receipt of the approved SEF budget by the Municipal Accountant, Municipal Treasurer, and Municipal Budget Officer be properly documented through preparation of transmittal letters or by indicating a signed and dated receipt of acknowledgment on the face of the approved SEF budget to ensure compliance with the seven-day submission requirement and improve transparency and accountability in the budgeting process and execution.

We recommended that the Local School Board (LSB) submit the quarterly SEF utilization reports to the DepEd Central Office, through the DepEd-Division of Bohol, copy furnished the local sanggunian, DBM and DILG Regional Offices, as mandated. We further recommended that an officer be designated for monitoring compliance with submission deadlines to ensure proper accountability and transparency in the utilization of the Special Education Fund.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management, in order to reflect in the books accurate amounts for the RPT and SET Receivables, require the Municipal Treasurer to prepare a duly certified list showing the name of taxpayers and the amount due and collectible for the year which data should come from the Real Property Tax Account Register (RPTAR) or Taxpayer’s index card maintained at the Municipal Treasurer’s Office and furnish the Municipal Accountant copy of the list to serve as the basis in recording the Real Property Tax Receivable and Special Education Tax Receivable accounts in the books.  This is in view of the need to record in the books not mere estimates from real property taxes but actual receivables from said taxes pursuant to Sections 19 and 20 of the NGAS Manual, Volume I.
observations.reason: Records showed that the LGU set up the receivable equal to the amount collected

observations.recommendation: We recommend that management submit the cash advance disbursement vouchers and supporting documents and justification on the non-submission of the cash advance voucher for post audit. observations.recommendation:
Recommendation:

      We recommend that management require the Municipal Treasurer  and  the
Municipal Accountant to  look  into  the  different  reconciling  items  and
effect appropriate adjustments where necessary.  We further  recommend  that
management notify the concerned bank on the erroneous entries made  by  them
and that adjustments of reconciling items be effected on a timely manner  to
ensure that Cash in Bank balances are accurately presented in the  financial
statements.

Recommendation:

      We  recommend  that  management  direct  the  Municipal  Treasurer  to
observe strict controls in the collection and  deposit  of  cash  such  that
their vault shall not hold  a  substantial  amount  overnight.   We  further
recommend that management judiciously plan their programs and activities  so
that  all  affected  departments  shall  be  well-coordinated  and  not  one
function be sacrificed over the benefit of another.

Recommendation:

      We recommend that management require the Municipal Treasurer  to  send
out notices to the  various  payees  whose  checks  have  become  stale  and
facilitate the cancellation or replacement thereof.   We  further  recommend
that the Municipal Accountant prepare the necessary JEV for the  restoration
of the amount in the cash account.

Recommendation:

      We recommend that management follow the requirements in the  granting,
utilization, monitoring, liquidation and accounting of  the  funds  released
as financial assistance to NGOs/POs and require the immediate submission  of
the related fund utilization/liquidation reports.

Recommendation:

             We  recommend  that  the  Municipal  Accountant  book   up   as
receivable, financial assistance  granted  to  barangays  and  NGOs/POs  and
record as expense only upon  its  proper  liquidation  by  the  implementing
agency or organization to ensure that  funds  are  spent  for  its  intended
public purpose only.

Recommendation:

      We recommend that management  closely monitor  and  ensure  that  the
LDRRM planned  program/projects/activities  are  implemented  and  give  due
regard on the maximum utilization of the 70% for pre  disaster  preparedness
programs  so  that   intended  purpose   is   attained/achieved.    Further,
management is reminded that the Quick Response Fund is used only for  relief
and recovery programs for communities or areas stricken by  disasters,
calamities, epidemics or complex emergencies.

We recommend that the Municipal Accountant compute and consistently provide the correct depreciation for all the properties of the Municipality periodically to ensure fair presentation of depreciation and related accounts in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: Depreciation was not provided for  properties in 2016
observations.reason: “Market” was depreciated in 2016 since it was subject to demolition

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Accountant prepare correcting entry to record the unexpended/unobligated balance of the LDRRMF under the Trust Fund books as Trust Liabilities – Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund (Code 2-04-01-020).

We recommend that the Municipal Accountant prepare the Monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF and submit the report on or before the 15th day after the end of each month through the MDRRMO to the COA auditor.
observations.reason: Only the year-end report was submitted

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the local government unit discontinue the practice of allocating discretionary funds to the Members of the Sangguniang Bayan in its Annual Budget. Any similar unconstitutional methods and mechanisms should not be adopted in any system of governance, by any name or form, in compliance with the Supreme Court decision promulgated on November 19, 2013.

We recommend that management require the Municipal Treasurer to keep and maintain a check register where all checks drawn for the day, including cancelled ones, are immediately recorded in chronological sequence and where the rightful claimants are required to acknowledge receipt of the check thereon. Further, cancelled checks should be physically presented to prove that these checks were actually unissued.

We recommend that management stop the practice of acquiring goods on a staggered basis thru shopping and adhere to the provisions of Section 10, Article IV of Republic Act 9184, which requires that all procurement shall be done through competitive public bidding to ensure that procurement of goods and services are obtained at prices which are most advantageous to the government.  Limit the use of alternative mode of procurement only when circumstances warrant and fall within the provisions of Section 52, Rule XVI of RA 9184.

We recommend that management require the concerned municipal drivers to completely and properly fill-up the driver’s trip tickets every time the government vehicles are used to comply with Section 361 (e) of the Government Accounting and Auditing Manual (GAAM) and pertinent section of COA Circular No.75-6 in order to properly monitor and control gasoline consumption.  

We further recommend that a charge invoice or its equivalent be given to the driver at the time of re-fuel which would then be used to counter-check the appropriate billing from the supplier.

We recommend that management submit the lacking documents indicated in this memorandum.  We further recommend that management comply with the provisions specified under Sections 168 and 362 of the GAAM Manual Volume I and that all prospective claims relative to repairs and maintenance of motor vehicles be properly supported with pertinent documents to substantiate their validity, propriety and legality.  Furthermore, we recommend that each vehicle be provided with a ledger/card that would be updated for all repairs and maintenance works pertaining to the specific vehicle.
observations.reason: Some claims for repairs and maintenance lacked the necessary documents to support such claims.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Accountant conduct investigation on the subsidiary ledgers comprising the various liability accounts with negative balances and prepare the necessary adjustments where necessary.  We further recommend that management honor the purpose for which trust funds were granted by the donors and use them only for the specific reasons these were granted, otherwise management has to seek authority from the donor to use the funds for other purposes.
observations.managementAction: Adjustments were made to most of the liability accounts with negative balances
